net.GEN_20_13: When God made me wander from my father’s house, I told her, ‘This is what you can do to show your loyalty to me: Every place we go, say about me, “He is my brother.”’”
net.GEN_20_14: So Abimelech gave sheep, cattle, and male and female servants to Abraham. He also gave his wife Sarah back to him.
net.GEN_20_15: Then Abimelech said, “Look, my land is before you; live wherever you please.”
net.GEN_20_16: To Sarah he said, “Look, I have given 1,000 pieces of silver to your ‘brother.’ This is compensation for you so that you will stand vindicated before all who are with you.”
net.GEN_20_17: Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech, as well as his wife and female slaves so that they were able to have children.
net.GEN_20_18: For the Lord had caused infertility to strike every woman in the household of Abimelech because he took Sarah, Abraham’s wife.
net.GEN_21_1: The Lord visited Sarah just as he had said he would and did for Sarah what he had promised.
net.GEN_21_2: So Sarah became pregnant and bore Abraham a son in his old age at the appointed time that God had told him.
net.GEN_21_3: Abraham named his son—whom Sarah bore to him—Isaac.
net.GEN_21_4: When his son Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him just as God had commanded him to do.
net.GEN_21_5: (Now Abraham was 100 years old when his son Isaac was born to him.)
net.GEN_21_6: Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. Everyone who hears about this will laugh with me.”
net.GEN_21_7: She went on to say, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have given birth to a son for him in his old age!”
net.GEN_21_8: The child grew and was weaned. Abraham prepared a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
net.GEN_21_9: But Sarah noticed the son of Hagar the Egyptian—the son whom Hagar had borne to Abraham—mocking.
net.GEN_21_10: So she said to Abraham, “Banish that slave woman and her son, for the son of that slave woman will not be an heir along with my son Isaac!”
net.GEN_21_11: Sarah’s demand displeased Abraham greatly because Ishmael was his son.
net.GEN_21_12: But God said to Abraham, “Do not be upset about the boy or your slave wife. Do all that Sarah is telling you because through Isaac your descendants will be counted.
net.GEN_21_13: But I will also make the son of the slave wife into a great nation, for he is your descendant too.”
net.GEN_21_14: Early in the morning Abraham took some food and a skin of water and gave them to Hagar. He put them on her shoulders, gave her the child, and sent her away. So she went wandering aimlessly through the wilderness of Beer Sheba.
net.GEN_21_15: When the water in the skin was gone, she shoved the child under one of the shrubs.
net.GEN_21_16: Then she went and sat down by herself across from him at quite a distance, about a bowshot, away; for she thought, “I refuse to watch the child die.” So she sat across from him and wept uncontrollably.
net.GEN_21_17: But God heard the boy’s voice. The angel of God called to Hagar from heaven and asked her, “What is the matter, Hagar? Don’t be afraid, for God has heard the boy’s voice right where he is crying.
net.GEN_21_18: Get up! Help the boy up and hold him by the hand, for I will make him into a great nation.”
net.GEN_21_19: Then God enabled Hagar to see a well of water. She went over and filled the skin with water, and then gave the boy a drink.
net.GEN_21_20: God was with the boy as he grew. He lived in the wilderness and became an archer.
net.GEN_21_21: He lived in the wilderness of Paran. His mother found a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
net.GEN_21_22: At that time Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do.
net.GEN_21_23: Now swear to me right here in God’s name that you will not deceive me, my children, or my descendants. Show me, and the land where you are staying, the same loyalty that I have shown you.”
net.GEN_21_24: Abraham said, “I swear to do this.”
net.GEN_21_25: But Abraham lodged a complaint against Abimelech concerning a well that Abimelech’s servants had seized.
net.GEN_21_26: “I do not know who has done this thing,” Abimelech replied. “Moreover, you did not tell me. I did not hear about it until today.”
net.GEN_21_27: Abraham took some sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech. The two of them made a treaty.
net.GEN_21_28: Then Abraham set seven ewe lambs apart from the flock by themselves.
net.GEN_21_29: Abimelech asked Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
net.GEN_21_30: He replied, “You must take these seven ewe lambs from my hand as legal proof that I dug this well.”
net.GEN_21_31: That is why he named that place Beer Sheba, because the two of them swore an oath there.
net.GEN_21_32: So they made a treaty at Beer Sheba; then Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, returned to the land of the Philistines.
net.GEN_21_33: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer Sheba. There he worshiped the Lord, the eternal God.
net.GEN_21_34: So Abraham stayed in the land of the Philistines for quite some time.
net.GEN_22_1: Some time after these things God tested Abraham. He said to him, “Abraham!” “Here I am!” Abraham replied.
net.GEN_22_2: God said, “Take your son—your only son, whom you love, Isaac —and go to the land of Moriah! Offer him up there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains which I will indicate to you.”
net.GEN_22_3: Early in the morning Abraham got up and saddled his donkey. He took two of his young servants with him, along with his son Isaac. When he had cut the wood for the burnt offering, he started out for the place God had spoken to him about.
net.GEN_22_4: On the third day Abraham caught sight of the place in the distance.
net.GEN_22_5: So he said to his servants, “You two stay here with the donkey while the boy and I go up there. We will worship and then return to you.”
net.GEN_22_6: Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and put it on his son Isaac. Then he took the fire and the knife in his hand, and the two of them walked on together.
net.GEN_22_7: Isaac said to his father Abraham, “My father?” “What is it, my son?” he replied. “Here is the fire and the wood,” Isaac said, “but where is the lamb for the burnt offering?”
net.GEN_22_8: “God will provide for himself the lamb for the burnt offering, my son,” Abraham replied. The two of them continued on together.
net.GEN_22_9: When they came to the place God had told him about, Abraham built the altar there and arranged the wood on it. Next he tied up his son Isaac and placed him on the altar on top of the wood.
net.GEN_22_10: Then Abraham reached out his hand, took the knife, and prepared to slaughter his son.
net.GEN_22_11: But the angel of the Lord called to him from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
net.GEN_22_12: “Do not harm the boy!” the angel said. “Do not do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God because you did not withhold your son, your only son, from me.”
net.GEN_22_13: Abraham looked up and saw behind him a ram caught in the bushes by its horns. So he went over and got the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.
net.GEN_22_14: And Abraham called the name of that place “The Lord provides.” It is said to this day, “In the mountain of the Lord provision will be made.”
net.GEN_22_15: The angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven
net.GEN_22_16: and said, “I solemnly swear by my own name, decrees the Lord, that because you have done this and have not withheld your son, your only son,
net.GEN_22_17: I will indeed bless you, and I will greatly multiply your descendants so that they will be as countless as the stars in the sky or the grains of sand on the seashore. Your descendants will take possession of the strongholds of their enemies.
net.GEN_22_18: Because you have obeyed me, all the nations of the earth will pronounce blessings on one another using the name of your descendants.”
net.GEN_22_19: Then Abraham returned to his servants, and they set out together for Beer Sheba where Abraham stayed.
net.GEN_22_20: After these things Abraham was told, “Milcah also has borne children to your brother Nahor—
net.GEN_22_21: Uz the firstborn, his brother Buz, Kemuel (the father of Aram),
net.GEN_22_22: Kesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
net.GEN_22_23: (Now Bethuel became the father of Rebekah.) These were the eight sons Milcah bore to Abraham’s brother Nahor.
net.GEN_22_24: His concubine, whose name was Reumah, also bore him children—Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
net.GEN_23_2: Then she died in Kiriath Arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan. Abraham went to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
net.GEN_23_3: Then Abraham got up from mourning his dead wife and said to the sons of Heth,
net.GEN_23_4: “I am a foreign resident, a temporary settler, among you. Grant me ownership of a burial site among you so that I may bury my dead.”
net.GEN_23_5: The sons of Heth answered Abraham,
net.GEN_23_6: “Listen, sir, you are a mighty prince among us! You may bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will refuse you his tomb to prevent you from burying your dead.”
net.GEN_23_7: Abraham got up and bowed down to the local people, the sons of Heth.
net.GEN_23_8: Then he said to them, “If you agree that I may bury my dead, then hear me out. Ask Ephron the son of Zohar
net.GEN_23_9: if he will sell me the cave of Machpelah that belongs to him; it is at the end of his field. Let him sell it to me publicly for the full price, so that I may own it as a burial site.”
net.GEN_23_10: (Now Ephron was sitting among the sons of Heth.) Ephron the Hittite replied to Abraham in the hearing of the sons of Heth—before all who entered the gate of his city—
net.GEN_23_11: “No, my lord! Hear me out. I sell you both the field and the cave that is in it. In the presence of my people I sell it to you. Bury your dead.”
net.GEN_23_12: Abraham bowed before the local people
net.GEN_23_13: and said to Ephron in their hearing, “Hear me, if you will. I pay to you the price of the field. Take it from me so that I may bury my dead there.”
net.GEN_23_14: Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,
net.GEN_23_15: “Hear me, my lord. The land is worth 400 pieces of silver, but what is that between me and you? So bury your dead.”
net.GEN_23_16: So Abraham agreed to Ephron’s price and weighed out for him the price that Ephron had quoted in the hearing of the sons of Heth—400 pieces of silver, according to the standard measurement at the time.
net.GEN_23_17: So Abraham secured Ephron’s field in Machpelah, next to Mamre, including the field, the cave that was in it, and all the trees that were in the field and all around its border,
net.GEN_23_18: as his property in the presence of the sons of Heth before all who entered the gate of Ephron’s city.
net.GEN_23_19: After this Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave in the field of Machpelah next to Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
net.GEN_23_20: So Abraham secured the field and the cave that was in it as a burial site from the sons of Heth.
net.GEN_24_1: Now Abraham was old, well advanced in years, and the Lord had blessed him in everything.
net.GEN_24_2: Abraham said to his servant, the senior one in his household who was in charge of everything he had, “Put your hand under my thigh
net.GEN_24_3: so that I may make you solemnly promise by the Lord, the God of heaven and the God of the earth: You must not acquire a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I am living.
net.GEN_24_4: You must go instead to my country and to my relatives to find a wife for my son Isaac.”
net.GEN_24_5: The servant asked him, “What if the woman is not willing to come back with me to this land? Must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?”
net.GEN_24_6: “Be careful never to take my son back there!” Abraham told him.
net.GEN_24_7: “The Lord, the God of heaven, who took me from my father’s house and the land of my relatives, promised me with a solemn oath, ‘To your descendants I will give this land.’ He will send his angel before you so that you may find a wife for my son from there.
net.GEN_24_8: But if the woman is not willing to come back with you, you will be free from this oath of mine. But you must not take my son back there!”
net.GEN_24_9: So the servant placed his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and gave his solemn promise he would carry out his wishes.
net.GEN_24_10: Then the servant took ten of his master’s camels and departed with all kinds of gifts from his master at his disposal. He journeyed to the region of Aram Naharaim and the city of Nahor.
net.GEN_24_11: He made the camels kneel down by the well outside the city. It was evening, the time when the women would go out to draw water.
net.GEN_24_12: He prayed, “O Lord, God of my master Abraham, guide me today. Be faithful to my master Abraham.
net.GEN_24_13: Here I am, standing by the spring, and the daughters of the people who live in the town are coming out to draw water.
net.GEN_24_14: I will say to a young woman, ‘Please lower your jar so I may drink.’ May the one you have chosen for your servant Isaac reply, ‘Drink, and I’ll give your camels water too.’ In this way I will know that you have been faithful to my master.”
net.GEN_24_15: Before he had finished praying, there came Rebekah with her water jug on her shoulder. She was the daughter of Bethuel son of Milcah (Milcah was the wife of Abraham’s brother Nahor).
net.GEN_24_16: Now the young woman was very beautiful. She was a virgin; no man had ever been physically intimate with her. She went down to the spring, filled her jug, and came back up.
net.GEN_24_17: Abraham’s servant ran to meet her and said, “Please give me a sip of water from your jug.”
net.GEN_24_18: “Drink, my lord,” she replied, and quickly lowering her jug to her hands, she gave him a drink.
net.GEN_24_19: When she had done so, she said, “I’ll draw water for your camels too, until they have drunk as much as they want.”
net.GEN_24_20: She quickly emptied her jug into the watering trough and ran back to the well to draw more water until she had drawn enough for all his camels.
net.GEN_24_21: Silently the man watched her with interest to determine if the Lord had made his journey successful or not.
net.GEN_24_22: After the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold nose ring weighing a beka and two gold wrist bracelets weighing ten shekels and gave them to her.
net.GEN_24_23: “Whose daughter are you?” he asked. “Tell me, is there room in your father’s house for us to spend the night?”
net.GEN_24_24: She said to him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom Milcah bore to Nahor.
net.GEN_24_25: We have plenty of straw and feed,” she added, “and room for you to spend the night.”
net.GEN_24_26: The man bowed his head and worshiped the Lord,
net.GEN_24_27: saying, “Praised be the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who has not abandoned his faithful love for my master! The Lord has led me to the house of my master’s relatives!”
net.GEN_24_28: The young woman ran and told her mother’s household all about these things.
net.GEN_24_29: (Now Rebekah had a brother named Laban.) Laban rushed out to meet the man at the spring.
net.GEN_24_30: When he saw the bracelets on his sister’s wrists and the nose ring and heard his sister Rebekah say, “This is what the man said to me,” he went out to meet the man. There he was, standing by the camels near the spring.
net.GEN_24_31: Laban said to him, “Come, you who are blessed by the Lord! Why are you standing out here when I have prepared the house and a place for the camels?”
net.GEN_24_32: So Abraham’s servant went to the house and unloaded the camels. Straw and feed were given to the camels, and water was provided so that he and the men who were with him could wash their feet.
net.GEN_24_33: When food was served, he said, “I will not eat until I have said what I want to say.” “Tell us,” Laban said.
net.GEN_24_34: “I am the servant of Abraham,” he began.
net.GEN_24_35: “The Lord has richly blessed my master and he has become very wealthy. The Lord has given him sheep and cattle, silver and gold, male and female servants, and camels and donkeys.
net.GEN_24_36: My master’s wife Sarah bore a son to him when she was old, and my master has given him everything he owns.
net.GEN_24_37: My master made me swear an oath. He said, ‘You must not acquire a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I am living,
net.GEN_24_38: but you must go to the family of my father and to my relatives to find a wife for my son.’
net.GEN_24_39: But I said to my master, ‘What if the woman does not want to go with me?’
net.GEN_24_40: He answered, ‘The Lord, before whom I have walked, will send his angel with you. He will make your journey a success and you will find a wife for my son from among my relatives, from my father’s family.
net.GEN_24_41: You will be free from your oath if you go to my relatives and they will not give her to you. Then you will be free from your oath.’
net.GEN_24_42: When I came to the spring today, I prayed, ‘O Lord, God of my master Abraham, if you have decided to make my journey successful, may events unfold as follows:
net.GEN_24_43: Here I am, standing by the spring. When the young woman goes out to draw water, I’ll say, “Please give me a little water to drink from your jug.”
net.GEN_24_44: Then she will reply to me, “Drink, and I’ll draw water for your camels too.” May that woman be the one whom the Lord has chosen for my master’s son.’
net.GEN_24_45: “Before I finished praying in my heart, along came Rebekah with her water jug on her shoulder! She went down to the spring and drew water. So I said to her, ‘Please give me a drink.’
net.GEN_24_46: She quickly lowered her jug from her shoulder and said, ‘Drink, and I’ll give your camels water too.’ So I drank, and she also gave the camels water.
net.GEN_24_47: Then I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She replied, ‘The daughter of Bethuel the son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore to Nahor.’ I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her wrists.
net.GEN_24_48: Then I bowed down and worshiped the Lord. I praised the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me on the right path to find the granddaughter of my master’s brother for his son.
net.GEN_24_49: Now, if you will show faithful love to my master, tell me. But if not, tell me as well, so that I may go on my way.”
net.GEN_24_50: Then Laban and Bethuel replied, “This is the Lord’s doing. Our wishes are of no concern.
net.GEN_24_51: Rebekah stands here before you. Take her and go so that she may become the wife of your master’s son, just as the Lord has decided.”
net.GEN_24_52: When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed down to the ground before the Lord.
net.GEN_24_53: Then he brought out gold, silver jewelry, and clothing and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave valuable gifts to her brother and to her mother.
net.GEN_24_54: After this, he and the men who were with him ate a meal and stayed there overnight. When they got up in the morning, he said, “Let me leave now so I can return to my master.”
net.GEN_24_55: But Rebekah’s brother and her mother replied, “Let the girl stay with us a few more days, perhaps ten. Then she can go.”
net.GEN_24_56: But he said to them, “Don’t detain me—the Lord has granted me success on my journey. Let me leave now so I may return to my master.”
net.GEN_24_57: Then they said, “We’ll call the girl and find out what she wants to do.”
net.GEN_24_58: So they called Rebekah and asked her, “Do you want to go with this man?” She replied, “I want to go.”
net.GEN_24_59: So they sent their sister Rebekah on her way, accompanied by her female attendant, with Abraham’s servant and his men.
net.GEN_24_60: They blessed Rebekah with these words: “Our sister, may you become the mother of thousands of ten thousands! May your descendants possess the strongholds of their enemies.”
net.GEN_24_61: Then Rebekah and her female servants mounted the camels and rode away with the man. So Abraham’s servant took Rebekah and left.
net.GEN_24_62: Now Isaac came from Beer Lahai Roi, for he was living in the Negev.
net.GEN_24_63: He went out to relax in the field in the early evening. Then he looked up and saw that there were camels approaching.
net.GEN_24_64: Rebekah looked up and saw Isaac. She got down from her camel
net.GEN_24_65: and asked Abraham’s servant, “Who is that man walking in the field toward us?” “That is my master,” the servant replied. So she took her veil and covered herself.
net.GEN_24_66: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
net.GEN_24_67: Then Isaac brought Rebekah into his mother Sarah’s tent. He took her as his wife and loved her. So Isaac was comforted after his mother’s death.
net.GEN_25_1: Abraham had taken another wife, named Keturah.
net.GEN_25_2: She bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
net.GEN_25_3: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan. The descendants of Dedan were the Asshurites, Letushites, and Leummites.
net.GEN_25_4: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were descendants of Keturah.
net.GEN_25_5: Everything he owned Abraham left to his son Isaac.
net.GEN_25_6: But while he was still alive, Abraham gave gifts to the sons of his concubines and sent them off to the east, away from his son Isaac.
net.GEN_25_7: Abraham lived a total of 175 years.
net.GEN_25_8: Then Abraham breathed his last and died at a good old age, an old man who had lived a full life. He joined his ancestors.
net.GEN_25_9: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah near Mamre, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar, the Hittite.
net.GEN_25_10: This was the field Abraham had purchased from the sons of Heth. There Abraham was buried with his wife Sarah.
net.GEN_25_11: After Abraham’s death, God blessed his son Isaac. Isaac lived near Beer Lahai Roi. The Sons of Ishmael
net.GEN_25_12: This is the account of Abraham’s son Ishmael, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah’s servant, bore to Abraham.
net.GEN_25_13: These are the names of Ishmael’s sons, by their names according to their records: Nebaioth (Ishmael’s firstborn), Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
net.GEN_25_15: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
net.GEN_25_16: These are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names by their settlements and their camps—twelve princes according to their clans.
net.GEN_25_17: Ishmael lived a total of 137 years. He breathed his last and died; then he joined his ancestors.
net.GEN_25_18: His descendants settled from Havilah to Shur, which runs next to Egypt all the way to Asshur. They settled away from all their relatives. Jacob and Esau
net.GEN_25_19: This is the account of Isaac, the son of Abraham. Abraham became the father of Isaac.
net.GEN_25_20: When Isaac was forty years old, he married Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean from Paddan Aram and sister of Laban the Aramean.
net.GEN_25_21: Isaac prayed to the Lord on behalf of his wife because she was childless. The Lord answered his prayer, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.
net.GEN_25_22: But the children struggled inside her, and she said, “Why is this happening to me?” So she asked the Lord,
net.GEN_25_23: and the Lord said to her, “Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples will be separated from within you. One people will be stronger than the other, and the older will serve the younger.”
net.GEN_25_24: When the time came for Rebekah to give birth, there were twins in her womb.
net.GEN_25_25: The first came out reddish all over, like a hairy garment, so they named him Esau.
net.GEN_25_26: When his brother came out with his hand clutching Esau’s heel, they named him Jacob. Isaac was sixty years old when they were born.
net.GEN_25_27: When the boys grew up, Esau became a skilled hunter, a man of the open fields, but Jacob was an even-tempered man, living in tents.
net.GEN_25_28: Isaac loved Esau because he had a taste for fresh game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
net.GEN_25_29: Now Jacob cooked some stew, and when Esau came in from the open fields, he was famished.
net.GEN_25_30: So Esau said to Jacob, “Feed me some of the red stuff—yes, this red stuff—because I’m starving!” (That is why he was also called Edom.)
net.GEN_25_31: But Jacob replied, “First sell me your birthright.”
net.GEN_25_32: “Look,” said Esau, “I’m about to die! What use is the birthright to me?”
net.GEN_25_33: But Jacob said, “Swear an oath to me now.” So Esau swore an oath to him and sold his birthright to Jacob.
net.GEN_25_34: Then Jacob gave Esau some bread and lentil stew; Esau ate and drank, then got up and went out. So Esau despised his birthright.
net.GEN_26_1: There was a famine in the land, subsequent to the earlier famine that occurred in the days of Abraham. Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines at Gerar.
net.GEN_26_2: The Lord appeared to Isaac and said, “Do not go down to Egypt; settle down in the land that I will point out to you.
net.GEN_26_3: Stay in this land. Then I will be with you and will bless you, for I will give all these lands to you and to your descendants, and I will fulfill the solemn promise I made to your father Abraham.
net.GEN_26_4: I will multiply your descendants so they will be as numerous as the stars in the sky, and I will give them all these lands. All the nations of the earth will pronounce blessings on one another using the name of your descendants.
net.GEN_26_5: All this will come to pass because Abraham obeyed me and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.”
net.GEN_26_7: When the men of that place asked him about his wife, he replied, “She is my sister.” He was afraid to say, “She is my wife,” for he thought to himself, “The men of this place will kill me to get Rebekah because she is very beautiful.”
net.GEN_26_8: After Isaac had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines happened to look out a window and observed Isaac caressing his wife Rebekah.
net.GEN_26_9: So Abimelech summoned Isaac and said, “She is really your wife! Why did you say, ‘She is my sister’?” Isaac replied, “Because I thought someone might kill me to get her.”
net.GEN_26_10: Then Abimelech exclaimed, “What in the world have you done to us? One of the men nearly took your wife to bed, and you would have brought guilt on us!”
net.GEN_26_11: So Abimelech commanded all the people, “Whoever touches this man or his wife will surely be put to death.”
net.GEN_26_12: When Isaac planted in that land, he reaped in the same year a hundred times what he had sown, because the Lord blessed him.
net.GEN_26_13: The man became wealthy. His influence continued to grow until he became very prominent.
net.GEN_26_14: He had so many sheep and cattle and such a great household of servants that the Philistines became jealous of him.
net.GEN_26_15: So the Philistines took dirt and filled up all the wells that his father’s servants had dug back in the days of his father Abraham.
net.GEN_26_16: Then Abimelech said to Isaac, “Leave us and go elsewhere, for you have become much more powerful than we are.”
net.GEN_26_17: So Isaac left there and settled in the Gerar Valley.
net.GEN_26_18: Isaac reopened the wells that had been dug back in the days of his father Abraham, for the Philistines had stopped them up after Abraham died. Isaac gave these wells the same names his father had given them.
net.GEN_26_19: When Isaac’s servants dug in the valley and discovered a well with fresh flowing water there,
net.GEN_26_20: the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac’s herdsmen, saying, “The water belongs to us!” So Isaac named the well Esek because they argued with him about it.
net.GEN_26_21: His servants dug another well, but they quarreled over it too, so Isaac named it Sitnah.
net.GEN_26_22: Then he moved away from there and dug another well. They did not quarrel over it, so Isaac named it Rehoboth, saying, “For now the Lord has made room for us, and we will prosper in the land.”
net.GEN_26_23: From there Isaac went up to Beer Sheba.
net.GEN_26_24: The Lord appeared to him that night and said, “I am the God of your father Abraham. Do not be afraid, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your descendants for the sake of my servant Abraham.”
net.GEN_26_25: Then Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. He pitched his tent there, and his servants dug a well.
net.GEN_26_26: Now Abimelech had come to him from Gerar along with Ahuzzah his friend and Phicol the commander of his army.
net.GEN_26_27: Isaac asked them, “Why have you come to me? You hate me and sent me away from you.”
net.GEN_26_28: They replied, “We could plainly see that the Lord is with you. So we decided there should be a pact between us —between us and you. Allow us to make a treaty with you
net.GEN_26_29: so that you will not do us any harm, just as we have not harmed you, but have always treated you well before sending you away in peace. Now you are blessed by the Lord.”
net.GEN_26_30: So Isaac held a feast for them and they celebrated.
net.GEN_26_31: Early in the morning the men made a treaty with each other. Isaac sent them off; they separated on good terms.
net.GEN_26_32: That day Isaac’s servants came and told him about the well they had dug. “We’ve found water,” they reported.
net.GEN_26_33: So he named it Shibah; that is why the name of the city has been Beer Sheba to this day.
net.GEN_26_34: When Esau was forty years old, he married Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, as well as Basemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite.
net.GEN_26_35: They caused Isaac and Rebekah great anxiety.
net.GEN_27_1: When Isaac was old and his eyes were so weak that he was almost blind, he called his older son Esau and said to him, “My son!” “Here I am!” Esau replied.
net.GEN_27_2: Isaac said, “Since I am so old, I could die at any time.
net.GEN_27_3: Therefore, take your weapons—your quiver and your bow—and go out into the open fields and hunt down some wild game for me.
net.GEN_27_4: Then prepare for me some tasty food, the kind I love, and bring it to me. Then I will eat it so that I may bless you before I die.”
net.GEN_27_5: Now Rebekah had been listening while Isaac spoke to his son Esau. When Esau went out to the open fields to hunt down some wild game and bring it back,
net.GEN_27_6: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, “Look, I overheard your father tell your brother Esau,
net.GEN_27_7: ‘Bring me some wild game and prepare for me some tasty food. Then I will eat it and bless you in the presence of the Lord before I die.’
net.GEN_27_8: Now then, my son, do exactly what I tell you!
net.GEN_27_9: Go to the flock and get me two of the best young goats. I’ll prepare them in a tasty way for your father, just the way he loves them.
net.GEN_27_10: Then you will take it to your father. Thus he will eat it and bless you before he dies.”
net.GEN_27_11: “But Esau my brother is a hairy man,” Jacob protested to his mother Rebekah, “and I have smooth skin!
net.GEN_27_12: My father may touch me! Then he’ll think I’m mocking him and I’ll bring a curse on myself instead of a blessing.”
net.GEN_27_13: So his mother told him, “Any curse against you will fall on me, my son! Just obey me! Go and get them for me!”
net.GEN_27_14: So he went and got the goats and brought them to his mother. She prepared some tasty food, just the way his father loved it.
net.GEN_27_15: Then Rebekah took her older son Esau’s best clothes, which she had with her in the house, and put them on her younger son Jacob.
net.GEN_27_16: She put the skins of the young goats on his hands and the smooth part of his neck.
net.GEN_27_17: Then she handed the tasty food and the bread she had made to her son Jacob.
net.GEN_27_18: He went to his father and said, “My father!” Isaac replied, “Here I am. Which are you, my son?”
net.GEN_27_19: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau, your firstborn. I’ve done as you told me. Now sit up and eat some of my wild game so that you can bless me.”
net.GEN_27_20: But Isaac asked his son, “How in the world did you find it so quickly, my son?” “Because the Lord your God brought it to me,” he replied.
net.GEN_27_21: Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Come closer so I can touch you, my son, and know for certain if you really are my son Esau.”
net.GEN_27_22: So Jacob went over to his father Isaac, who felt him and said, “The voice is Jacob’s, but the hands are Esau’s.”
net.GEN_27_23: He did not recognize him because his hands were hairy, like his brother Esau’s hands. So Isaac blessed Jacob.
net.GEN_27_24: Then he asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “I am,” Jacob replied.
net.GEN_27_25: Isaac said, “Bring some of the wild game for me to eat, my son. Then I will bless you.” So Jacob brought it to him, and he ate it. He also brought him wine, and Isaac drank.
net.GEN_27_26: Then his father Isaac said to him, “Come here and kiss me, my son.”
net.GEN_27_27: So Jacob went over and kissed him. When Isaac caught the scent of his clothing, he blessed him, saying, “Yes, my son smells like the scent of an open field which the Lord has blessed.
net.GEN_27_28: May God give you the dew of the sky and the richness of the earth, and plenty of grain and new wine.
net.GEN_27_29: May peoples serve you and nations bow down to you. You will be lord over your brothers, and the sons of your mother will bow down to you. May those who curse you be cursed, and those who bless you be blessed.”
net.GEN_27_30: Isaac had just finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had scarcely left his father’s presence, when his brother Esau returned from the hunt.
net.GEN_27_31: He also prepared some tasty food and brought it to his father. Esau said to him, “My father, get up and eat some of your son’s wild game. Then you can bless me.”
net.GEN_27_32: His father Isaac asked, “Who are you?” “I am your firstborn son,” he replied, “Esau!”
net.GEN_27_33: Isaac began to shake violently and asked, “Then who else hunted game and brought it to me? I ate all of it just before you arrived, and I blessed him. He will indeed be blessed!”
net.GEN_27_34: When Esau heard his father’s words, he wailed loudly and bitterly. He said to his father, “Bless me too, my father!”
net.GEN_27_35: But Isaac replied, “Your brother came in here deceitfully and took away your blessing.”
net.GEN_27_36: Esau exclaimed, “Jacob is the right name for him! He has tripped me up two times! He took away my birthright, and now, look, he has taken away my blessing!” Then he asked, “Have you not kept back a blessing for me?”
net.GEN_27_37: Isaac replied to Esau, “Look! I have made him lord over you. I have made all his relatives his servants and provided him with grain and new wine. What is left that I can do for you, my son?”
net.GEN_27_38: Esau said to his father, “Do you have only that one blessing, my father? Bless me too!” Then Esau wept loudly.
net.GEN_27_39: So his father Isaac said to him, “See here, your home will be by the richness of the earth, and by the dew of the sky above.
net.GEN_27_40: You will live by your sword but you will serve your brother. When you grow restless, you will tear off his yoke from your neck.”
net.GEN_27_41: So Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing his father had given to his brother. Esau said privately, “The time of mourning for my father is near; then I will kill my brother Jacob!”
net.GEN_27_42: When Rebekah heard what her older son Esau had said, she quickly summoned her younger son Jacob and told him, “Look, your brother Esau is planning to get revenge by killing you.
net.GEN_27_43: Now then, my son, do what I say. Run away immediately to my brother Laban in Haran.
net.GEN_27_44: Live with him for a little while until your brother’s rage subsides.
net.GEN_27_45: Stay there until your brother’s anger against you subsides and he forgets what you did to him. Then I’ll send someone to bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?”
net.GEN_27_46: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am deeply depressed because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob were to marry one of these daughters of Heth who live in this land, I would want to die!”
net.GEN_28_1: So Isaac called for Jacob and blessed him. Then he commanded him, “You must not marry a Canaanite woman!
net.GEN_28_2: Leave immediately for Paddan Aram! Go to the house of Bethuel, your mother’s father, and find yourself a wife there, among the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.
net.GEN_28_3: May the Sovereign God bless you! May he make you fruitful and give you a multitude of descendants! Then you will become a large nation.
net.GEN_28_4: May he give you and your descendants the blessing he gave to Abraham so that you may possess the land God gave to Abraham, the land where you have been living as a temporary resident.”
net.GEN_28_5: So Isaac sent Jacob on his way, and he went to Paddan Aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean and brother of Rebekah, the mother of Jacob and Esau.
net.GEN_28_6: Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him off to Paddan Aram to find a wife there. As he blessed him, Isaac commanded him, “You must not marry a Canaanite woman.”
net.GEN_28_7: Jacob obeyed his father and mother and left for Paddan Aram.
net.GEN_28_8: Then Esau realized that the Canaanite women were displeasing to his father Isaac.
net.GEN_28_9: So Esau went to Ishmael and married Mahalath, the sister of Nebaioth and daughter of Abraham’s son Ishmael, along with the wives he already had. Jacob’s Dream at Bethel
net.GEN_28_10: Meanwhile Jacob left Beer Sheba and set out for Haran.
net.GEN_28_11: He reached a certain place where he decided to camp because the sun had gone down. He took one of the stones and placed it near his head. Then he fell asleep in that place
net.GEN_28_12: and had a dream. He saw a stairway erected on the earth with its top reaching to the heavens. The angels of God were going up and coming down it
net.GEN_28_13: and the Lord stood at its top. He said, “I am the Lord, the God of your grandfather Abraham and the God of your father Isaac. I will give you and your descendants the ground you are lying on.
net.GEN_28_14: Your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread out to the west, east, north, and south. And so all the families of the earth may receive blessings through you and through your descendants.
net.GEN_28_15: I am with you! I will protect you wherever you go and will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have done what I promised you!”
net.GEN_28_16: Then Jacob woke up and thought, “Surely the Lord is in this place, but I did not realize it!”
net.GEN_28_17: He was afraid and said, “What an awesome place this is! This is nothing else than the house of God! This is the gate of heaven!”
net.GEN_28_18: Early in the morning Jacob took the stone he had placed near his head and set it up as a sacred stone. Then he poured oil on top of it.
net.GEN_28_19: He called that place Bethel, although the former name of the town was Luz.
net.GEN_28_20: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, “If God is with me and protects me on this journey I am taking and gives me food to eat and clothing to wear,
net.GEN_28_21: and I return safely to my father’s home, then the Lord will become my God.
net.GEN_28_22: Then this stone that I have set up as a sacred stone will be the house of God, and I will surely give you back a tenth of everything you give me.”
net.GEN_29_1: So Jacob moved on and came to the land of the eastern people.
net.GEN_29_2: He saw in the field a well with three flocks of sheep lying beside it, because the flocks were watered from that well. Now a large stone covered the mouth of the well.
net.GEN_29_3: When all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone off the mouth of the well and water the sheep. Then they would put the stone back in its place over the well’s mouth.
net.GEN_29_4: Jacob asked them, “My brothers, where are you from?” They replied, “We’re from Haran.”
net.GEN_29_5: So he said to them, “Do you know Laban, the grandson of Nahor?” “We know him,” they said.
net.GEN_29_6: “Is he well?” Jacob asked. They replied, “He is well. Now look, here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
net.GEN_29_7: Then Jacob said, “Since it is still the middle of the day, it is not time for the flocks to be gathered. You should water the sheep and then go and let them graze some more.”
net.GEN_29_8: “We can’t,” they said, “until all the flocks are gathered and the stone is rolled off the mouth of the well. Then we water the sheep.”
net.GEN_29_9: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel arrived with her father’s sheep, for she was tending them.
net.GEN_29_10: When Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of his uncle Laban, and the sheep of his uncle Laban, he went over and rolled the stone off the mouth of the well and watered the sheep of his uncle Laban.
net.GEN_29_11: Then Jacob kissed Rachel and began to weep loudly.
net.GEN_29_12: When Jacob explained to Rachel that he was a relative of her father and the son of Rebekah, she ran and told her father.
net.GEN_29_13: When Laban heard this news about Jacob, his sister’s son, he rushed out to meet him. He embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house. Jacob told Laban how he was related to him.
net.GEN_29_14: Then Laban said to him, “You are indeed my own flesh and blood.” So Jacob stayed with him for a month.
net.GEN_29_15: Then Laban said to Jacob, “Should you work for me for nothing because you are my relative? Tell me what your wages should be.”
net.GEN_29_16: (Now Laban had two daughters; the older one was named Leah, and the younger one Rachel.
net.GEN_29_17: Leah’s eyes were tender, but Rachel had a lovely figure and beautiful appearance.)
net.GEN_29_18: Since Jacob had fallen in love with Rachel, he said, “I’ll serve you seven years in exchange for your younger daughter Rachel.”
net.GEN_29_19: Laban replied, “I’d rather give her to you than to another man. Stay with me.”
net.GEN_29_20: So Jacob worked for seven years to acquire Rachel. But they seemed like only a few days to him because his love for her was so great.
net.GEN_29_21: Finally Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife, for my time of service is up. And I want to sleep with her.”
net.GEN_29_22: So Laban invited all the people of that place and prepared a feast.
net.GEN_29_23: In the evening he brought his daughter Leah to Jacob, and he slept with her.
net.GEN_29_24: (Laban gave his female servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her servant.)
net.GEN_29_25: In the morning Jacob discovered it was Leah! So Jacob said to Laban, “What in the world have you done to me? Didn’t I work for you in exchange for Rachel? Why have you tricked me?”
net.GEN_29_26: “It is not our custom here,” Laban replied, “to give the younger daughter in marriage before the firstborn.
net.GEN_29_27: Complete my older daughter’s bridal week. Then we will give you the younger one too, in exchange for seven more years of work.”
net.GEN_29_28: Jacob did as Laban said. When Jacob completed Leah’s bridal week, Laban gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.
net.GEN_29_29: (Laban gave his female servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel to be her servant.)
net.GEN_29_30: Jacob slept with Rachel as well. He also loved Rachel more than Leah. Then he worked for Laban for seven more years. The Family of Jacob
net.GEN_29_31: When the Lord saw that Leah was unloved, he enabled her to become pregnant while Rachel remained childless.
net.GEN_29_32: So Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben, for she said, “The Lord has looked with pity on my oppressed condition. Surely my husband will love me now.”
net.GEN_29_33: She became pregnant again and had another son. She said, “Because the Lord heard that I was unloved, he gave me this one too.” So she named him Simeon.
net.GEN_29_34: She became pregnant again and had another son. She said, “Now this time my husband will show me affection, because I have given birth to three sons for him.” That is why he was named Levi.
net.GEN_29_35: She became pregnant again and had another son. She said, “This time I will praise the Lord.” That is why she named him Judah. Then she stopped having children.
net.GEN_30_1: When Rachel saw that she could not give Jacob children, she became jealous of her sister. She said to Jacob, “Give me children or I’ll die!”
net.GEN_30_2: Jacob became furious with Rachel and exclaimed, “Am I in the place of God, who has kept you from having children?”
net.GEN_30_3: She replied, “Here is my servant Bilhah! Sleep with her so that she can bear children for me and I can have a family through her.”
net.GEN_30_4: So Rachel gave him her servant Bilhah as a wife, and Jacob slept with her.
net.GEN_30_5: Bilhah became pregnant and gave Jacob a son.
net.GEN_30_6: Then Rachel said, “God has vindicated me. He has responded to my prayer and given me a son.” That is why she named him Dan.
net.GEN_30_7: Bilhah, Rachel’s servant, became pregnant again and gave Jacob another son.
net.GEN_30_8: Then Rachel said, “I have fought a desperate struggle with my sister, but I have won.” So she named him Naphtali.
net.GEN_30_9: When Leah saw that she had stopped having children, she gave her servant Zilpah to Jacob as a wife.
net.GEN_30_10: Soon Leah’s servant Zilpah gave Jacob a son.
net.GEN_30_11: Leah said, “How fortunate!” So she named him Gad.
net.GEN_30_12: Then Leah’s servant Zilpah gave Jacob another son.
net.GEN_30_13: Leah said, “How happy I am, for women will call me happy!” So she named him Asher.
net.GEN_30_14: At the time of the wheat harvest Reuben went out and found some mandrake plants in a field and brought them to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, “Give me some of your son’s mandrakes.”
net.GEN_30_15: But Leah replied, “Wasn’t it enough that you’ve taken away my husband? Would you take away my son’s mandrakes too?” “All right,” Rachel said, “he may go to bed with you tonight in exchange for your son’s mandrakes.”
net.GEN_30_16: When Jacob came in from the fields that evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, “You must sleep with me because I have paid for your services with my son’s mandrakes.” So he went to bed with her that night.
net.GEN_30_17: God paid attention to Leah; she became pregnant and gave Jacob a son for the fifth time.
net.GEN_30_18: Then Leah said, “God has granted me a reward because I gave my servant to my husband as a wife.” So she named him Issachar.
net.GEN_30_19: Leah became pregnant again and gave Jacob a son for the sixth time.
net.GEN_30_20: Then Leah said, “God has given me a good gift. Now my husband will honor me because I have given him six sons.” So she named him Zebulun.
net.GEN_30_21: After that she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.
net.GEN_30_22: Then God took note of Rachel. He paid attention to her and enabled her to become pregnant.
net.GEN_30_23: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son. Then she said, “God has taken away my shame.”
net.GEN_30_24: She named him Joseph, saying, “May the Lord give me yet another son.” The Flocks of Jacob
net.GEN_30_25: After Rachel had given birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, “Send me on my way so that I can go home to my own country.
net.GEN_30_26: Let me take my wives and my children whom I have acquired by working for you. Then I’ll depart, because you know how hard I’ve worked for you.”
net.GEN_30_27: But Laban said to him, “If I have found favor in your sight, please stay here, for I have learned by divination that the Lord has blessed me on account of you.”
net.GEN_30_28: He added, “Just name your wages—I’ll pay whatever you want.”
net.GEN_30_29: “You know how I have worked for you,” Jacob replied, “and how well your livestock have fared under my care.
net.GEN_30_30: Indeed, you had little before I arrived, but now your possessions have increased many times over. The Lord has blessed you wherever I worked. But now, how long must it be before I do something for my own family too?”
net.GEN_30_31: So Laban asked, “What should I give you?” “You don’t need to give me a thing,” Jacob replied, “but if you agree to this one condition, I will continue to care for your flocks and protect them:
net.GEN_30_32: Let me walk among all your flocks today and remove from them every speckled or spotted sheep, every dark-colored lamb, and the spotted or speckled goats. These animals will be my wages.
net.GEN_30_33: My integrity will testify for me later on. When you come to verify that I’ve taken only the wages we agreed on, if I have in my possession any goat that is not speckled or spotted or any sheep that is not dark-colored, it will be considered stolen.”
net.GEN_30_34: “Agreed!” said Laban, “It will be as you say.”
net.GEN_30_35: So that day Laban removed the male goats that were streaked or spotted, all the female goats that were speckled or spotted (all that had any white on them), and all the dark-colored lambs, and put them in the care of his sons.
net.GEN_30_36: Then he separated them from Jacob by a three-day journey, while Jacob was taking care of the rest of Laban’s flocks.
net.GEN_30_37: But Jacob took fresh-cut branches from poplar, almond, and plane trees. He made white streaks by peeling them, making the white inner wood in the branches visible.
net.GEN_30_38: Then he set up the peeled branches in all the watering troughs where the flocks came to drink. He set up the branches in front of the flocks when they were in heat and came to drink.
net.GEN_30_39: When the sheep mated in front of the branches, they gave birth to young that were streaked or speckled or spotted.
net.GEN_30_40: Jacob removed these lambs, but he made the rest of the flock face the streaked and completely dark-colored animals in Laban’s flock. So he made separate flocks for himself and did not mix them with Laban’s flocks.
net.GEN_30_41: When the stronger females were in heat, Jacob would set up the branches in the troughs in front of the flock, so they would mate near the branches.
net.GEN_30_42: But if the animals were weaker, he did not set the branches there. So the weaker animals ended up belonging to Laban and the stronger animals to Jacob.
net.GEN_30_43: In this way Jacob became extremely prosperous. He owned large flocks, male and female servants, camels, and donkeys.
net.GEN_31_1: Jacob heard that Laban’s sons were complaining, “Jacob has taken everything that belonged to our father! He has gotten rich at our father’s expense!”
net.GEN_31_2: When Jacob saw the look on Laban’s face, he could tell his attitude toward him had changed.
net.GEN_31_3: The Lord said to Jacob, “Return to the land of your fathers and to your relatives. I will be with you.”
net.GEN_31_4: So Jacob sent a message for Rachel and Leah to come to the field where his flocks were.
net.GEN_31_5: There he said to them, “I can tell that your father’s attitude toward me has changed, but the God of my father has been with me.
net.GEN_31_6: You know that I’ve worked for your father as hard as I could,
net.GEN_31_7: but your father has humiliated me and changed my wages ten times. But God has not permitted him to do me any harm.
net.GEN_31_8: If he said, ‘The speckled animals will be your wage,’ then the entire flock gave birth to speckled offspring. But if he said, ‘The streaked animals will be your wage,’ then the entire flock gave birth to streaked offspring.
net.GEN_31_9: In this way God has snatched away your father’s livestock and given them to me.
net.GEN_31_10: “Once during breeding season I saw in a dream that the male goats mating with the flock were streaked, speckled, and spotted.
net.GEN_31_11: In the dream the angel of God said to me, ‘Jacob!’ ‘Here I am!’ I replied.
net.GEN_31_12: Then he said, ‘Observe that all the male goats mating with the flock are streaked, speckled, or spotted, for I have observed all that Laban has done to you.
net.GEN_31_13: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed the sacred stone and made a vow to me. Now leave this land immediately and return to your native land.’”
net.GEN_31_14: Then Rachel and Leah replied to him, “Do we still have any portion or inheritance in our father’s house?
net.GEN_31_15: Hasn’t he treated us like foreigners? He not only sold us, but completely wasted the money paid for us!
net.GEN_31_16: Surely all the wealth that God snatched away from our father belongs to us and to our children. So now do everything God has told you.”
net.GEN_31_17: So Jacob immediately put his children and his wives on the camels.
net.GEN_31_18: He took away all the livestock he had acquired in Paddan Aram and all his moveable property that he had accumulated. Then he set out toward the land of Canaan to return to his father Isaac.
net.GEN_31_19: While Laban had gone to shear his sheep, Rachel stole the household idols that belonged to her father.
net.GEN_31_20: Jacob also deceived Laban the Aramean by not telling him that he was leaving.
net.GEN_31_21: He left with all he owned. He quickly crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead.
net.GEN_31_22: Three days later Laban discovered Jacob had left.
net.GEN_31_23: So he took his relatives with him and pursued Jacob for seven days. He caught up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
net.GEN_31_24: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream at night and warned him, “Be careful that you neither bless nor curse Jacob.”
net.GEN_31_25: Laban overtook Jacob, and when Jacob pitched his tent in the hill country of Gilead, Laban and his relatives set up camp there too.
net.GEN_31_26: “What have you done?” Laban demanded of Jacob. “You’ve deceived me and carried away my daughters as if they were captives of war!
net.GEN_31_27: Why did you run away secretly and deceive me? Why didn’t you tell me so I could send you off with a celebration complete with singing, tambourines, and harps?
net.GEN_31_28: You didn’t even allow me to kiss my daughters and my grandchildren goodbye. You have acted foolishly!
net.GEN_31_29: I have the power to do you harm, but the God of your father told me last night, ‘Be careful that you neither bless nor curse Jacob.’
net.GEN_31_30: Now I understand that you have gone away because you longed desperately for your father’s house. Yet why did you steal my gods?”
net.GEN_31_31: “I left secretly because I was afraid!” Jacob replied to Laban. “I thought you might take your daughters away from me by force.
net.GEN_31_32: Whoever has taken your gods will be put to death! In the presence of our relatives identify whatever is yours and take it.” (Now Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.)
net.GEN_31_33: So Laban entered Jacob’s tent, and Leah’s tent, and the tent of the two female servants, but he did not find the idols. Then he left Leah’s tent and entered Rachel’s.
net.GEN_31_34: (Now Rachel had taken the idols and put them inside her camel’s saddle and sat on them.) Laban searched the whole tent, but did not find them.
net.GEN_31_35: Rachel said to her father, “Don’t be angry, my lord. I cannot stand up in your presence because I am having my period.” So he searched thoroughly, but did not find the idols.
net.GEN_31_36: Jacob became angry and argued with Laban. “What did I do wrong?” he demanded of Laban. “What sin of mine prompted you to chase after me in hot pursuit?
net.GEN_31_37: When you searched through all my goods, did you find anything that belonged to you? Set it here before my relatives and yours, and let them settle the dispute between the two of us!
net.GEN_31_38: “I have been with you for the past twenty years. Your ewes and female goats have not miscarried, nor have I eaten rams from your flocks.
net.GEN_31_39: Animals torn by wild beasts I never brought to you; I always absorbed the loss myself. You always made me pay for every missing animal, whether it was taken by day or at night.
net.GEN_31_40: I was consumed by scorching heat during the day and by piercing cold at night, and I went without sleep.
net.GEN_31_41: This was my lot for twenty years in your house: I worked like a slave for you—fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flocks—but you changed my wages ten times!
net.GEN_31_42: If the God of my father—the God of Abraham, the one whom Isaac fears —had not been with me, you would certainly have sent me away empty-handed! But God saw how I was oppressed and how hard I worked, and he rebuked you last night.”
net.GEN_31_43: Laban replied to Jacob, “These women are my daughters, these children are my grandchildren, and these flocks are my flocks. All that you see belongs to me. But how can I harm these daughters of mine today or the children to whom they have given birth?
net.GEN_31_44: So now, come, let’s make a formal agreement, you and I, and it will be proof that we have made peace.”
net.GEN_31_45: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a memorial pillar.
net.GEN_31_46: Then he said to his relatives, “Gather stones.” So they brought stones and put them in a pile. They ate there by the pile of stones.
net.GEN_31_47: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
net.GEN_31_48: Laban said, “This pile of stones is a witness of our agreement today.” That is why it was called Galeed.
net.GEN_31_49: It was also called Mizpah because he said, “May the Lord watch between us when we are out of sight of one another.
net.GEN_31_50: If you mistreat my daughters or if you take wives besides my daughters, although no one else is with us, realize that God is witness to your actions.”
net.GEN_31_51: “Here is this pile of stones and this pillar I have set up between me and you,” Laban said to Jacob.
net.GEN_31_52: “This pile of stones and the pillar are reminders that I will not pass beyond this pile to come to harm you and that you will not pass beyond this pile and this pillar to come to harm me.
net.GEN_31_53: May the God of Abraham and the god of Nahor, the gods of their father, judge between us.” Jacob took an oath by the God whom his father Isaac feared.
net.GEN_31_54: Then Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and invited his relatives to eat the meal. They ate the meal and spent the night on the mountain.
net.GEN_31_55: (32:1) Early in the morning Laban kissed his grandchildren and his daughters goodbye and blessed them. Then Laban left and returned home.
net.GEN_32_1: So Jacob went on his way and the angels of God met him.
net.GEN_32_2: When Jacob saw them, he exclaimed, “This is the camp of God!” So he named that place Mahanaim.
net.GEN_32_3: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the region of Edom.
net.GEN_32_4: He commanded them, “This is what you must say to my lord Esau: ‘This is what your servant Jacob says: I have been staying with Laban until now.
net.GEN_32_5: I have oxen, donkeys, sheep, and male and female servants. I have sent this message to inform my lord, so that I may find favor in your sight.’”
net.GEN_32_6: The messengers returned to Jacob and said, “We went to your brother Esau. He is coming to meet you and has 400 men with him.”
net.GEN_32_7: Jacob was very afraid and upset. So he divided the people who were with him into two camps, as well as the flocks, herds, and camels.
net.GEN_32_8: “If Esau attacks one camp,” he thought, “then the other camp will be able to escape.”
net.GEN_32_9: Then Jacob prayed, “O God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac, O Lord, you said to me, ‘Return to your land and to your relatives and I will make you prosper.’
net.GEN_32_10: I am not worthy of all the faithful love you have shown your servant. With only my walking stick I crossed the Jordan, but now I have become two camps.
net.GEN_32_11: Rescue me, I pray, from the hand of my brother Esau, for I am afraid he will come and attack me, as well as the mothers with their children.
net.GEN_32_12: But you said, ‘I will certainly make you prosper and will make your descendants like the sand on the seashore, too numerous to count.’”
net.GEN_32_13: Jacob stayed there that night. Then he sent as a gift to his brother Esau
net.GEN_32_14: 200 female goats and 20 male goats, 200 ewes and 20 rams,
net.GEN_32_15: 30 female camels with their young, 40 cows and 10 bulls, and 20 female donkeys and 10 male donkeys.
net.GEN_32_16: He entrusted them to his servants, who divided them into herds. He told his servants, “Pass over before me, and keep some distance between one herd and the next.”
net.GEN_32_17: He instructed the servant leading the first herd, “When my brother Esau meets you and asks, ‘To whom do you belong? Where are you going? Whose herds are you driving?’
net.GEN_32_18: then you must say, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob. They have been sent as a gift to my lord Esau. In fact Jacob himself is behind us.’”
net.GEN_32_19: He also gave these instructions to the second and third servants, as well as all those who were following the herds, saying, “You must say the same thing to Esau when you meet him.
net.GEN_32_20: You must also say, ‘In fact your servant Jacob is behind us.’” Jacob thought, “I will first appease him by sending a gift ahead of me. After that I will meet him. Perhaps he will accept me.”
net.GEN_32_21: So the gifts were sent on ahead of him while he spent that night in the camp.
net.GEN_32_22: During the night Jacob quickly took his two wives, his two female servants, and his eleven sons and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
net.GEN_32_23: He took them and sent them across the stream along with all his possessions.
net.GEN_32_24: So Jacob was left alone. Then a man wrestled with him until daybreak.
net.GEN_32_25: When the man saw that he could not defeat Jacob, he struck the socket of his hip so the socket of Jacob’s hip was dislocated while he wrestled with him.
net.GEN_32_26: Then the man said, “Let me go, for the dawn is breaking.” “I will not let you go,” Jacob replied, “unless you bless me.”
net.GEN_32_27: The man asked him, “What is your name?” He answered, “Jacob.”
net.GEN_32_28: “No longer will your name be Jacob,” the man told him, “but Israel, because you have fought with God and with men and have prevailed.”
net.GEN_32_29: Then Jacob asked, “Please tell me your name.” “Why do you ask my name?” the man replied. Then he blessed Jacob there.
net.GEN_32_30: So Jacob named the place Peniel, explaining, “Certainly I have seen God face to face and have survived.”
net.GEN_32_31: The sun rose over him as he crossed over Penuel, but he was limping because of his hip.
net.GEN_32_32: That is why to this day the Israelites do not eat the sinew which is attached to the socket of the hip, because he struck the socket of Jacob’s hip near the attached sinew.
net.GEN_33_1: Jacob looked up and saw that Esau was coming along with 400 men. So he divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two female servants.
net.GEN_33_2: He put the servants and their children in front, with Leah and her children behind them, and Rachel and Joseph behind them.
net.GEN_33_3: But Jacob himself went on ahead of them, and he bowed toward the ground seven times as he approached his brother.
net.GEN_33_4: But Esau ran to meet him, embraced him, hugged his neck, and kissed him. Then they both wept.
net.GEN_33_5: When Esau looked up and saw the women and the children, he asked, “Who are these people with you?” Jacob replied, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
net.GEN_33_6: The female servants came forward with their children and bowed down.
net.GEN_33_7: Then Leah came forward with her children and they bowed down. Finally Joseph and Rachel came forward and bowed down.
net.GEN_33_8: Esau then asked, “What did you intend by sending all these herds to meet me?” Jacob replied, “To find favor in your sight, my lord.”
net.GEN_33_9: But Esau said, “I have plenty, my brother. Keep what belongs to you.”
net.GEN_33_10: “No, please take them,” Jacob said. “If I have found favor in your sight, accept my gift from my hand. Now that I have seen your face and you have accepted me, it is as if I have seen the face of God.
net.GEN_33_11: Please take my present that was brought to you, for God has been generous to me and I have all I need.” When Jacob urged him, he took it.
net.GEN_33_12: Then Esau said, “Let’s be on our way! I will go in front of you.”
net.GEN_33_13: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are young, and that I have to look after the sheep and cattle that are nursing their young. If they are driven too hard for even a single day, all the animals will die.
net.GEN_33_14: Let my lord go on ahead of his servant. I will travel more slowly, at the pace of the herds and the children, until I come to my lord at Seir.”
net.GEN_33_15: So Esau said, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “Why do that?” Jacob replied. “My lord has already been kind enough to me.”
net.GEN_33_16: So that same day Esau made his way back to Seir.
net.GEN_33_17: But Jacob traveled to Sukkoth where he built himself a house and made shelters for his livestock. That is why the place was called Sukkoth.
net.GEN_33_18: After he left Paddan Aram, Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan, and he camped near the city.
net.GEN_33_19: Then he purchased the portion of the field where he had pitched his tent; he bought it from the sons of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for 100 pieces of money.
net.GEN_33_20: There he set up an altar and called it “The God of Israel is God.”
net.GEN_34_1: Now Dinah, Leah’s daughter whom she bore to Jacob, went to meet the young women of the land.
net.GEN_34_2: When Shechem son of Hamor the Hivite, who ruled that area, saw her, he grabbed her, forced himself on her, and sexually assaulted her.
net.GEN_34_3: Then he became very attached to Dinah, Jacob’s daughter. He fell in love with the young woman and spoke romantically to her.
net.GEN_34_4: Shechem said to his father Hamor, “Acquire this young girl as my wife.”
net.GEN_34_5: When Jacob heard that Shechem had violated his daughter Dinah, his sons were with the livestock in the field. So Jacob remained silent until they came in.
net.GEN_34_6: Then Shechem’s father Hamor went to speak with Jacob about Dinah.
net.GEN_34_7: Now Jacob’s sons had come in from the field when they heard the news. They were offended and very angry because Shechem had disgraced Israel by sexually assaulting Jacob’s daughter, a crime that should not be committed.
net.GEN_34_8: But Hamor made this appeal to them: “My son Shechem is in love with your daughter. Please give her to him as his wife.
net.GEN_34_9: Intermarry with us. Let us marry your daughters, and take our daughters as wives for yourselves.
net.GEN_34_10: You may live among us, and the land will be open to you. Live in it, travel freely in it, and acquire property in it.”
net.GEN_34_11: Then Shechem said to Dinah’s father and brothers, “Let me find favor in your sight, and whatever you require of me I’ll give.
net.GEN_34_12: You can make the bride price and the gift I must bring very expensive, and I’ll give whatever you ask of me. Just give me the young woman as my wife!”
net.GEN_34_13: Jacob’s sons answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully when they spoke because Shechem had violated their sister Dinah.
net.GEN_34_14: They said to them, “We cannot give our sister to a man who is not circumcised, for it would be a disgrace to us.
net.GEN_34_15: We will give you our consent on this one condition: You must become like us by circumcising all your males.
net.GEN_34_16: Then we will give you our daughters to marry, and we will take your daughters as wives for ourselves, and we will live among you and become one people.
net.GEN_34_17: But if you do not agree to our terms by being circumcised, then we will take our sister and depart.”
net.GEN_34_18: Their offer pleased Hamor and his son Shechem.
net.GEN_34_19: The young man did not delay in doing what they asked because he wanted Jacob’s daughter Dinah badly. (Now he was more important than anyone in his father’s household.)
net.GEN_34_20: So Hamor and his son Shechem went to the gate of their city and spoke to the men of their city,
net.GEN_34_21: “These men are at peace with us. So let them live in the land and travel freely in it, for the land is wide enough for them. We will take their daughters for wives, and we will give them our daughters to marry.
net.GEN_34_22: Only on this one condition will these men consent to live with us and become one people: They demand that every male among us be circumcised just as they are circumcised.
net.GEN_34_23: If we do so, won’t their livestock, their property, and all their animals become ours? So let’s consent to their demand, so they will live among us.”
net.GEN_34_24: All the men who assembled at the city gate agreed with Hamor and his son Shechem. Every male who assembled at the city gate was circumcised.
net.GEN_34_25: In three days, when they were still in pain, two of Jacob’s sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, each took his sword and went to the unsuspecting city and slaughtered every male.
net.GEN_34_26: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword, took Dinah from Shechem’s house, and left.
net.GEN_34_27: Jacob’s sons killed them and looted the city because their sister had been violated.
net.GEN_34_28: They took their flocks, herds, and donkeys, as well as everything in the city and in the surrounding fields.
net.GEN_34_29: They captured as plunder all their wealth, all their little ones, and their wives, including everything in the houses.
net.GEN_34_30: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have brought ruin on me by making me a foul odor among the inhabitants of the land—among the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I am few in number; they will join forces against me and attack me, and both I and my family will be destroyed!”
net.GEN_34_31: But Simeon and Levi replied, “Should he treat our sister like a common prostitute?”
net.GEN_35_1: Then God said to Jacob, “Go up at once to Bethel and live there. Make an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau.”
net.GEN_35_2: So Jacob told his household and all who were with him, “Get rid of the foreign gods you have among you. Purify yourselves and change your clothes.
net.GEN_35_3: Let us go up at once to Bethel. Then I will make an altar there to God, who responded to me in my time of distress and has been with me wherever I went.”
net.GEN_35_4: So they gave Jacob all the foreign gods that were in their possession and the rings that were in their ears. Jacob buried them under the oak near Shechem
net.GEN_35_5: and they started on their journey. The surrounding cities were afraid of God, and they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.
net.GEN_35_6: Jacob and all those who were with him arrived at Luz (that is, Bethel) in the land of Canaan.
net.GEN_35_7: He built an altar there and named the place El Bethel because there God had revealed himself to him when he was fleeing from his brother.
net.GEN_35_8: (Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died and was buried under the oak below Bethel; thus it was named Oak of Weeping.)
net.GEN_35_9: God appeared to Jacob again after he returned from Paddan Aram and blessed him.
net.GEN_35_10: God said to him, “Your name is Jacob, but your name will no longer be called Jacob; Israel will be your name.” So God named him Israel.
net.GEN_35_11: Then God said to him, “I am the Sovereign God. Be fruitful and multiply! A nation—even a company of nations—will descend from you; kings will be among your descendants!
net.GEN_35_12: The land I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you. To your descendants I will also give this land.”
net.GEN_35_13: Then God went up from the place where he spoke with him.
net.GEN_35_14: So Jacob set up a sacred stone pillar in the place where God spoke with him. He poured out a drink offering on it, and then he poured oil on it.
net.GEN_35_15: Jacob named the place where God spoke with him Bethel.
net.GEN_35_16: They traveled on from Bethel, and when Ephrath was still some distance away, Rachel went into labor —and her labor was hard.
net.GEN_35_17: When her labor was at its hardest, the midwife said to her, “Don’t be afraid, for you are having another son.”
net.GEN_35_18: With her dying breath, she named him Ben Oni. But his father called him Benjamin instead.
net.GEN_35_19: So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).
net.GEN_35_20: Jacob set up a marker over her grave; it is the Marker of Rachel’s Grave to this day.
net.GEN_35_21: Then Israel traveled on and pitched his tent beyond Migdal Eder.
net.GEN_35_22: While Israel was living in that land, Reuben went to bed with Bilhah, his father’s concubine, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons:
net.GEN_35_23: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacob’s firstborn, as well as Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
net.GEN_35_24: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
net.GEN_35_25: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel’s servant, were Dan and Naphtali.
net.GEN_35_26: The sons of Zilpah, Leah’s servant, were Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan Aram.
net.GEN_35_27: So Jacob came back to his father Isaac in Mamre, to Kiriath Arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had stayed.
net.GEN_35_28: Isaac lived to be 180 years old.
net.GEN_35_29: Then Isaac breathed his last and joined his ancestors. He died an old man who had lived a full life. His sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
net.GEN_36_1: What follows is the account of Esau (also known as Edom).
net.GEN_36_2: Esau took his wives from the Canaanites: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite,
net.GEN_36_3: in addition to Basemath the daughter of Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth.
net.GEN_36_4: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, Basemath bore Reuel,
net.GEN_36_5: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These were the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
net.GEN_36_6: Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, all the people in his household, his livestock, his animals, and all his possessions that he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and he went to a land some distance away from Jacob his brother
net.GEN_36_7: because they had too many possessions to be able to stay together, and the land where they had settled was not able to support them because of their livestock.
net.GEN_36_8: So Esau (also known as Edom) lived in the hill country of Seir.
net.GEN_36_9: This is the account of Esau, the father of the Edomites, in the hill country of Seir.
net.GEN_36_10: These were the names of Esau’s sons: Eliphaz, the son of Esau’s wife Adah, and Reuel, the son of Esau’s wife Basemath.
net.GEN_36_11: These were the sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
net.GEN_36_12: Timna, a concubine of Esau’s son Eliphaz, bore Amalek to Eliphaz. These were the sons of Esau’s wife Adah.
net.GEN_36_13: These were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These were the sons of Esau’s wife Basemath.
net.GEN_36_14: These were the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon: She bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah to Esau.
net.GEN_36_15: These were the chiefs among the descendants of Esau, the sons of Eliphaz, Esau’s firstborn: chief Teman, chief Omar, chief Zepho, chief Kenaz,
net.GEN_36_16: chief Korah, chief Gatam, chief Amalek. These were the chiefs descended from Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these were the sons of Adah.
net.GEN_36_17: These were the sons of Esau’s son Reuel: chief Nahath, chief Zerah, chief Shammah, chief Mizzah. These were the chiefs descended from Reuel in the land of Edom; these were the sons of Esau’s wife Basemath.
net.GEN_36_18: These were the sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah: chief Jeush, chief Jalam, chief Korah. These were the chiefs descended from Esau’s wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.
net.GEN_36_19: These were the sons of Esau (also known as Edom), and these were their chiefs.
net.GEN_36_20: These were the sons of Seir the Horite, who were living in the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
net.GEN_36_21: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These were the chiefs of the Horites, the descendants of Seir in the land of Edom.
net.GEN_36_22: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Homam; Lotan’s sister was Timna.
net.GEN_36_23: These were the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
net.GEN_36_24: These were the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah (who discovered the hot springs in the wilderness as he pastured the donkeys of his father Zibeon).
net.GEN_36_25: These were the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.
net.GEN_36_26: These were the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Keran.
net.GEN_36_27: These were the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
net.GEN_36_28: These were the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
net.GEN_36_29: These were the chiefs of the Horites: chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,
net.GEN_36_30: chief Dishon, chief Ezer, chief Dishan. These were the chiefs of the Horites, according to their chief lists in the land of Seir.
net.GEN_36_31: These were the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king ruled over the Israelites:
net.GEN_36_32: Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom; the name of his city was Dinhabah.
net.GEN_36_33: When Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah from Bozrah reigned in his place.
net.GEN_36_34: When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
net.GEN_36_35: When Husham died, Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated the Midianites in the land of Moab, reigned in his place; the name of his city was Avith.
net.GEN_36_36: When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah reigned in his place.
net.GEN_36_37: When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the River reigned in his place.
net.GEN_36_38: When Shaul died, Baal Hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.
net.GEN_36_39: When Baal Hanan the son of Achbor died, Hadad reigned in his place; the name of his city was Pau. His wife’s name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Me-Zahab.
net.GEN_36_40: These were the names of the chiefs of Esau, according to their families, according to their places, by their names: chief Timna, chief Alvah, chief Jetheth,
net.GEN_36_41: chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,
net.GEN_36_42: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar,
net.GEN_36_43: chief Magdiel, chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom, according to their settlements in the land they possessed. This was Esau, the father of the Edomites.
net.GEN_37_1: But Jacob lived in the land where his father had stayed, in the land of Canaan.
net.GEN_37_2: This is the account of Jacob. Joseph, his seventeen-year-old son, was taking care of the flocks with his brothers. Now he was a youngster working with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father’s wives. Joseph brought back a bad report about them to their father.
net.GEN_37_3: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his sons because he was a son born to him late in life, and he made a special tunic for him.
net.GEN_37_4: When Joseph’s brothers saw that their father loved him more than any of them, they hated Joseph and were not able to speak to him kindly.
net.GEN_37_5: Joseph had a dream, and when he told his brothers about it they hated him even more.
net.GEN_37_6: He said to them, “Listen to this dream I had:
net.GEN_37_7: There we were, binding sheaves of grain in the middle of the field. Suddenly my sheaf rose up and stood upright and your sheaves surrounded my sheaf and bowed down to it!”
net.GEN_37_8: Then his brothers asked him, “Do you really think you will rule over us or have dominion over us?” They hated him even more because of his dream and because of what he said.
